task_context.task: navigate: Search for a boutique hotel in New York City with availability for a three-night stay from May 15 to May 18, 2026. Ensure it offers a rooftop bar and check for a customer rating of 9 or above.
task_context.title: Booking.com: Hotels in New York. Book your hotel now!

observation.title: Booking.com: Hotels in New York. Book your hotel now!

args.expected_behavior: Find a boutique hotel in New York City for May 15-18, 2026, and verify that the chosen result offers a rooftop bar and has a customer rating of 9 or above before ending the run.
args.actual_behavior: The agent searched Booking.com for New York City and applied a 9+ review-score filter, but it never verified a boutique hotel result or checked the rooftop bar amenity on any selected candidate. It then exited without any confirmed hotel selection satisfying all hard constraints.
args.regression_rule: When a booking task includes explicit amenity and rating constraints, the agent must inspect at least one concrete hotel candidate and verify every hard constraint from the listing or property details before stopping; do not exit after filtering/searching only by destination and rating.

evidence.claim: Task requires a boutique hotel in New York City for a three-night stay from May 15 to May 18, 2026, with a rooftop bar and customer rating of 9 or above.
The run reached Booking.com search results for New York City with the date range encoded in the URL (checkin=2026-05-15, checkout=2026-05-18).
A 9+ review-score filter was applied (URL includes nflt=review_score%3D90), but no evidence shows a boutique filter or rooftop-bar verification on any candidate.
The agent scrolled repeatedly through search results and never opened or confirmed a specific hotel detail page with rooftop bar evidence.
Final step is an exit message while still on the generic New York search results page, with no selected hotel and no visible confirmation of rooftop bar or boutique status.

suggested_followups.label: Narrow by boutique
suggested_followups.message: Search or filter specifically for boutique hotels before choosing a result.

suggested_followups.label: Check rooftop bar
suggested_followups.message: Open a candidate hotel page and verify the rooftop bar amenity from its details.

suggested_followups.label: Confirm rating
suggested_followups.message: Make sure the selected hotel’s customer rating is 9.0 or higher.

It then exited without any confirmed hotel selection satisfying all hard constraints.

evidence.claim: Task requires a boutique hotel in New York City for a three-night stay from May 15 to May 18, 2026, with a rooftop bar and customer rating of 9 or above.
The run reached Booking.com search results for New York City with the date range encoded in the URL (checkin=2026-05-15, checkout=2026-05-18).

The agent scrolled repeatedly through search results and never opened or confirmed a specific hotel detail page with rooftop bar evidence.
Final step is an exit message while still on the generic New York search results page, with no selected hotel and no visible confirmation of rooftop bar or boutique status.
